poster_title: LeanRAG: Knowledge-Graph-Based Generation with Semantic Aggregation and Hierarchical Retrieval
authors: Yaoze Zhang, Rong Wu, Pinlong Cai, Xiaoman Wang, Guohang Yan, Song Mao, Ding Wang, Botian Shi

content: RAG enhances LLMs by using external knowledge.
Naive RAG suffers from incomplete information retrieval.
Knowledge graph-based RAG improves local context preservation.
High-level summaries lack explicit relational connections.
Current retrieval is structurally unaware, leading to inefficiency.
Semantic aggregation forms entity clusters and constructs explicit relations.
Bottom-up retrieval anchors queries to relevant entities and traverses the graph systematically.
Creates a fully navigable semantic network.
Conducted experiments on four QA benchmarks.
Evaluated response quality and retrieval efficiency.
LeanRAG outperformed existing methods in response quality.
panels.figure_caption: Comparison of typical LLM retrieval-augmented generation frameworks.

content: Semantic aggregation preserves both fine-grained facts and high-level connections.
Structure-guided retrieval mitigates information redundancy and maximizes relevance.
Achieved state-of-the-art performance on multiple QA tasks.
LeanRAG addresses limitations of existing RAG methods.
Improves efficiency and quality of response generation in LLMs.

Fig1.caption: Comparison of typical LLM retrieval-augmented generation frameworks.
Fig1.description: 比较了典型的 LLM 检索增强生成框架。

Fig2.caption: Overview of the LeanRAG framework.
Fig2.description: 概述了 LeanRAG 框架的结构。
